plot.title: The Vanishing Sapphire
plot.content: During the Metropolitan Museum's gala on October 1, 2025, the priceless Sapphire Necklace vanished from its display after a sudden power outage. Emily Hart, the exhibit curator, discovered the loss when she resumed the inspection. Security logs show no badge swipe for the back‑door, yet only two staff members had spare keys: Lucas Reed, a junior security officer, and Maya Patel, a volunteer lobby attendant who was on duty that night. Emily, the victim, recalls hearing a hushed conversation between Lucas and Maya earlier in the evening. The investigation must determine who stole the necklace and why.

caseFiles.title: Email: Lucas to Maya
caseFiles.content: Hey Maya, thanks for covering the lobby shift. Remember to keep the badge handy for the after‑hours tour. We'll need a quick exit after the gala.

caseFiles.title: Message: Maya to Lucas
caseFiles.content: Lucas, I just saw you lingering by the Sapphire Exhibit when the lights flickered. You looked nervous.

caseFiles.title: Interview: Emily Hart
caseFiles.content: The necklace was gone when I checked the display after the power outage. Security logs show no badge swipe for the backdoor, but I recall Lucas was the only staff member with a spare key.

nodes.title: Email: Lucas to Maya
nodes.content: Hey Maya, thanks for covering the lobby shift. Remember to keep the badge handy for the after‑hours tour. We'll need a quick exit after the gala.

edges.title: Email: Lucas to Maya
edges.content: Hey Maya, thanks for covering the lobby shift. Remember to keep the badge handy for the after‑hours tour. We'll need a quick exit after the gala.

solution.motive: Lucas owed a large sum to a black‑market collector and stole the necklace to sell it and settle the debt.
